solution.p: So in set-builder notation, the solution set is {\{ i \mid i \lt 5 \}}\text{.}
We can graph this by shading all numbers less than 5\text{.} Since the sign in the inequality does not allow for i to equal 5\text{,} we use a parenthesis at 5\text{.}
We read the graph from left to right to tell us the interval notation for the solution set: {\left(-\infty ,5\right)}\text{.}
